title: Find All Duplicates in an Array
problem_statement: Given an array of integers where 1 ≤ nums[i] ≤ n (n = size of array), some elements appear twice and others appear once. Return all elements that appear twice.
constraints: Each integer appears once or twice.
intuition: Index-marking works because values map to indices. Negate the value at the mapped index to mark it seen; if it is already negative, the number is a duplicate.

approaches.name: Index Marking
approaches.description: Use each value as an index and flip the sign of the visited position. A second visit indicates a duplicate.

interview_qa.a: Yes, it negates numbers in place. If the original array must remain unchanged, use a HashMap instead.

interview_qa.q: Why is this safe for 1..n values?
interview_qa.a: The input guarantees values lie in the index range, so each value maps to a valid index.
